title: The Classification Head
concept: A small linear layer is added on top of the '[CLS]' output vector, trained to predict class categories using softmax.

exercises.prompt: Before we classify text, which token's output vector represents the summary of the entire sentence?

options.text: The punctuation mark at the end of a sentence
The special '[CLS]' token which represents the whole sequence
A random word chosen from the middle of the text

exercises.explain: In many models like BERT, the special '[CLS]' token is inserted at the beginning of the text to gather and store the overall meaning of the entire sentence.

cards.title: Making the Final Call
cards.body: Transformers are great at understanding context, but how do they actually make a final decision like "Spam" or "Not Spam"? They use a specialized ending piece called a classification head to turn abstract context into a concrete choice!

cards.title: What is a Classification Head?
cards.body: A classification head is a small neural network layer (usually a single linear layer) placed on top of a Transformer model. It takes the summary representation—specifically from the special '[CLS]' token—and converts it into class probabilities using a softmax function.

cards.title: The Mail Carrier
cards.body: Think of a post office. The Transformer encoder is the mail sorter that packages all the details of a letter into a single stamp on the front. The classification head is the mail carrier who looks only at that single stamp to decide which delivery truck (category) the letter belongs to.

cards.title: Classifying Movie Reviews
cards.body: If you are building a movie review classifier, the Transformer outputs a 768-number vector for the '[CLS]' token. The classification head takes this vector, multiplies it by weights to get 2 numbers (representing 'Positive' and 'Negative'), and applies softmax to give you probabilities like 92% Positive and 8% Negative.

cards.title: No Need to Rebuild
cards.body:
❌ Myth: You need to rewrite the entire Transformer model to make it classify text.
   
✅ Reality: The Transformer stays mostly the same! You only need to plug in a tiny, simple linear layer (the classification head) at the very end to do the final sorting.

cards.title: Heavy Processing vs. Final Decision
cards.body: Compare how the heavy context processing compares to the simple decision layer at the end.

right.text: Takes only the [CLS] vector and maps it directly to class probabilities.

left.text: Processes deep context across all words and outputs a summarized [CLS] vector.

exercises.prompt: Which specific part of the Transformer's output does the classification head look at?

options.text: Every single word's output vector combined
The output vector of the special '[CLS]' token
The raw input text before any layers process it
The attention weights matrix from the middle layer

exercises.explain: The [CLS] token output acts as a dense summary of the entire sentence, making it the perfect input for the classification head to analyze.

exercises.prompt: The classification head is added at the end of the network, after the main Transformer encoder has processed the text.
exercises.explain: The classification head is added at the very end of the model (on top of the output vectors) to perform the final classification task.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing component of the classification head:
exercises.sentence: To predict categories, the classification head feeds the [CLS] vector into a simple [blank] layer followed by a softmax function.
exercises.explain: A classification head uses a simple linear layer (often called a Dense layer) on top of the special [CLS] token to project the text representation into class scores.

exercises.prompt: Identify the correct description of a classification head:

options.text: It is a massive 12-layer decoder network that generates new text from scratch.
It is a simple linear layer that maps the summary vector to category probabilities using softmax.
It is a manual lookup table of keywords that counts positive and negative words.

exercises.explain: The AI response correctly identifies that the head is just a simple linear layer with a softmax function, whereas the humans wrongly think it is a huge or complex sequence generator.

exercises.prompt: Your team is building a spam filter using a pre-trained Transformer. You need to attach a classification head to get the final "Spam" or "Not Spam" label. How does this head process the information?

options.text: It reads the raw text input directly and outputs words.
It takes the [CLS] vector, passes it through a linear layer, and applies softmax.
It removes all the attention layers and replaces them with a decision tree.
It acts as a feedback loop that forces the model to rewrite the text.

exercises.explain: A classification head takes only the vector representing the entire sequence (like the [CLS] token output) and uses a linear layer + softmax to project it to the final class probabilities. It does not process the raw text directly or train the entire model from scratch.
